title: Placental Matrisome Proteomic Analysis by LC-MS/MS
author: Amanpreet K. Bains

steps.title: Tissue Homogenization
steps.instruction: Tissue Homogenization

steps.title: Verification of ECM Enrichment
steps.instruction: Verification of ECM Enrichment

steps.title: Reduction and Alkylation
steps.instruction: Reduction and Alkylation

steps.title: Deglycosylation and Proteolytic Digestion
steps.instruction: Deglycosylation and Proteolytic Digestion

steps.title: Instrumentation
steps.instruction: Instrumentation

steps.title: Liquid Chromatography
steps.instruction: Liquid Chromatography

steps.title: Mass Spectrometry Acquisition Parameters
steps.instruction: Mass Spectrometry Acquisition Parameters

steps.title: Weigh 45–120 mg of placenta tissue for each sample
steps.instruction: Weigh 45–120 mg of placenta tissue for each sample.

steps.title: Transfer tissue into bead homogenization tubes containing
steps.instruction: Transfer tissue into bead homogenization tubes containing: Four 2.8 mm ceramic beads (OMNI International #19628) Four 2.4 mm metal beads (OMNI International #19-610)

steps.title: Homogenize tissue using an OMNI Bead...
steps.instruction: Homogenize tissue using an OMNI Bead Ruptor at 8 m/s using: Three 10-second homogenization cycles 60-second pauses between cycles

steps.title: Assess enrichment efficiency and intracellular protein...
steps.instruction: Assess enrichment efficiency and intracellular protein depletion by western blot analysis.

steps.title: Probe samples using the following primary antibodies
steps.instruction: Probe samples using the following primary antibodies: Anti-actin (kindly gifted by Dr. Richard O. Hynes; use at 1:5000 dilution) Anti-integrin beta-1 (kindly gifted by Dr. Richard O. Hynes; use at 1:1000 dilution) Anti-collagen III (Abcam #ab7778; use at 2 µg/mL) Anti-GAPDH (Sigma #MAB374; use at 2 µg/mL) Anti-histone H4 (Sigma #05-858; use at 1:30000 dilution) Anti-transglutaminase 2/TGM2 (Abcam #ab421; use at 2.5 µg /mL)

steps.title: Detect primary antibodies using HRP-conjugated secondary...
steps.instruction: Detect primary antibodies using HRP-conjugated secondary antibodies: Goat anti-rabbit HRP (Invitrogen #31460; use at 8 µg /mL) Goat anti-mouse HRP (Invitrogen #31430; use at 1.6 µg/mL)

steps.title: Solubilize ECM-enriched protein pellets in 8...
steps.instruction: Solubilize ECM-enriched protein pellets in 8 M urea prepared in 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate.

steps.title: Reduce disulfide bonds using 10 mM DTT
steps.instruction: Reduce disulfide bonds using 10 mM DTT.

steps.title: Alkylate reduced cysteines using 25 mM...
steps.instruction: Alkylate reduced cysteines using 25 mM iodoacetamide for 30 minutes at room temperature in the dark.

steps.title: Dilute samples to a final urea concentration of 2 M
steps.instruction: Dilute samples to a final urea concentration of 2 M.

steps.title: Deglycosylate proteins using PNGase F for...
steps.instruction: Deglycosylate proteins using PNGase F for 2 hours at 37°C under mild agitation.

steps.title: Digest proteins with Lys-C for 2...
steps.instruction: Digest proteins with Lys-C for 2 hours at 37°C under mild agitation.

steps.title: Add trypsin and incubate samples overnight...
steps.instruction: Add trypsin and incubate samples overnight at 37°C under mild agitation.

steps.title: Add a fresh aliquot of trypsin...
steps.instruction: Add a fresh aliquot of trypsin the following day and incubate samples for an additional 2 hours at 37°C.

steps.title: Desalt peptides using Pierce Peptide Desalting...
steps.instruction: Desalt peptides using Pierce Peptide Desalting Spin Columns according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Lyophilize desalted peptides
steps.instruction: Lyophilize desalted peptides.

steps.title: Reconstitute peptides in
steps.instruction: Reconstitute peptides in: 95% HPLC-grade water 5% acetonitrile 0.1% formic acid

steps.title: Quantify peptide concentration using the Pierce...
steps.instruction: Quantify peptide concentration using the Pierce Quantitative Colorimetric Peptide Assay Kit (#23275).

steps.title: We perform LC-MS/MS analysis at the...
steps.instruction: We perform LC-MS/MS analysis at the University of Illinois Chicago Research Resources Center – Mass Spectrometry Core using the following instrumentation: Q Exactive HF mass spectrometer UltiMate 3000 RSLC nanosystem Nanospray Flex Ion Source

steps.title: Inject 1 µg peptide per sample for LC-MS/MS analysis
steps.instruction: Inject 1 µg peptide per sample for LC-MS/MS analysis.

steps.title: Load samples onto a Waters nanoEase M/Z C18 trap column
steps.instruction: Load samples onto a Waters nanoEase M/Z C18 trap column: 100 Å 5 µm 180 µm × 20 mm

steps.title: Separate peptides using a Waters BEH C18 analytical column
steps.instruction: Separate peptides using a Waters BEH C18 analytical column: 130 Å 1.7 µm 75 µm × 150 mm

steps.title: Perform chromatography at a flow rate of 300 nL/min
steps.instruction: Perform chromatography at a flow rate of 300 nL/min.

steps.title: Prepare mobile phases as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare mobile phases as follows: Solvent A: 0.1% formic acid in water Solvent B: 0.1% formic acid in 80% acetonitrile

steps.title: Run the following LC gradient
steps.instruction: Run the following LC gradient: 5% B from 0–3 min 8% B at 6 min 8–40% B from 6–85 min 40–95% B from 85–90 min Hold at 95% B until 94.8 min Re-equilibrate at 5% B until 105 min

steps.title: Acquire full MS survey scans over...
steps.instruction: Acquire full MS survey scans over a mass range of 350–1400 m/z at a resolution of 120,000.

steps.title: Set the AGC target for full MS scans to 3
steps.instruction: Set the AGC target for full MS scans to 3.0 × 10^6.

steps.title: Select the 20 most intense precursor...
steps.instruction: Select the 20 most intense precursor ions with charge states 2–5 for fragmentation.

steps.title: Fragment precursor ions using higher-energy collisional...
steps.instruction: Fragment precursor ions using higher-energy collisional dissociation (HCD) with a normalized collision energy of 28%.

steps.title: Apply dynamic exclusion for 30 seconds using a 1
steps.instruction: Apply dynamic exclusion for 30 seconds using a 1.2 m/z exclusion window.

steps.title: Acquire tandem MS/MS spectra at a resolution of 30,000
steps.instruction: Acquire tandem MS/MS spectra at a resolution of 30,000.

steps.title: Set the AGC target for MS/MS scans to 1
steps.instruction: Set the AGC target for MS/MS scans to 1.0 × 10^5.

steps.title: Use an ion selection threshold of 2
steps.instruction: Use an ion selection threshold of 2.0 × 10^4 counts.

steps.title: Set maximum ion injection times to
steps.instruction: Set maximum ion injection times to: 50 ms for full MS scans 120 ms for MS/MS scans

steps.title: Acidify peptide samples using 50% TFA
steps.instruction: Acidify peptide samples using 50% TFA.

steps.title: Perform sequential extraction of intracellular proteins...
steps.instruction: Perform sequential extraction of intracellular proteins using the Subcellular Protein Fractionation Kit for Tissues (Thermo Scientific #87790) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Sequentially remove intracellular proteins in order...
steps.instruction: Sequentially remove intracellular proteins in order of decreasing solubility to enrich extracellular matrix proteins.

steps.title: ECM Enrichment by Sequential Extraction A...
steps.instruction: ECM Enrichment by Sequential Extraction A summary of the steps used for ECM enrichment by sequential extraction is provided below. For more detailed instructions, refer to the following protocol: Compartmental Protein Extraction to Achieve Enrichment of Extracellular Matrix (ECM) Proteins by Alexandra Naba

description: Protein Solubilization A summary of the steps used for solubilization and in-solution digestion of ECM-enriched proteins is provided below. For more detailed instructions, refer to the following protocol: In-solution Digestion of ECM-Enriched Proteins Samples for Mass Spectrometry Analysis by Alexandra Naba Peptide Cleanup and Quantification A summary of the steps used for peptide desalting and quantification is provided below. For more detailed instructions, refer to the following protocol: Desalting of Peptides to Prepare for Mass Spectrometry Analysis by Alexandra Naba
